Chronology of every recorded fact about [[Elderspawn]], in reading order. Generated from the same verified fact store as the main article.

== Of Sea and Shadow ==
* {{spoiler|book=ee-sea-01-of-sea-and-shadow|'''Ch 1''' — An Elderspawn is a clawed creature that Calder had to nail to the inside of his hull.}}
* {{spoiler|book=ee-sea-01-of-sea-and-shadow|'''Ch 3''' — Elderspawn are creatures that may not get tired in the human sense.}}
* {{spoiler|book=ee-sea-01-of-sea-and-shadow|'''Ch 7''' — The volume of Elderspawn is a measure for loudness.}}
* {{spoiler|book=ee-sea-01-of-sea-and-shadow|'''Ch 7''' — The Elderspawn is an entity with awareness that can be read by Calder.}}
* {{spoiler|book=ee-sea-01-of-sea-and-shadow|'''Ch 9''' — Elderspawn act as guides for the Navigators to cross the Aion.}}
* {{spoiler|book=ee-sea-01-of-sea-and-shadow|'''Ch 9''' — The Sleepless might send an Elderspawn killer to eliminate a witness.}}
* {{spoiler|book=ee-sea-01-of-sea-and-shadow|'''Ch 10''' — Some Elderspawn lack heads, hearts, spines, or limbs.}}
* {{spoiler|book=ee-sea-01-of-sea-and-shadow|'''Ch 11''' — An Elderspawn may have developed the ability to command others.}}
* {{spoiler|book=ee-sea-01-of-sea-and-shadow|'''Ch 11''' — Andel did not want a blade that remembered being inside an Elderspawn.}}
* {{spoiler|book=ee-sea-01-of-sea-and-shadow|'''Ch 12''' — Elderspawn are immobilized when they make contact with Awakened iron.}}
* {{spoiler|book=ee-sea-01-of-sea-and-shadow|'''Ch 12''' — Elderspawn are monsters that attack the gates.}}
* {{spoiler|book=ee-sea-01-of-sea-and-shadow|'''Ch 15''' — The ruler of the Empire is described as an Elderspawned excuse for a ruler.}}
* {{spoiler|book=ee-sea-01-of-sea-and-shadow|'''Ch 18''' — An Elderspawn is a giant creature.}}
* {{spoiler|book=ee-sea-01-of-sea-and-shadow|'''Ch 19''' — Elderspawn are creatures that can bother ships unless an Elder is chained to them.}}
* {{spoiler|book=ee-sea-01-of-sea-and-shadow|'''Ch 19''' — Elderspawn are creatures that can be strange or alien in biology and mentality, but not necessarily malicious.}}
* {{spoiler|book=ee-sea-01-of-sea-and-shadow|'''Ch 23''' — Elderspawn may arrive during a ritual sequence.}}
* {{spoiler|book=ee-sea-01-of-sea-and-shadow|'''Ch 24''' — Elderspawn are beings that can be summoned through the application of Intent.}}
* {{spoiler|book=ee-sea-01-of-sea-and-shadow|'''Ch 24''' — The Elderspawn is a creature that can tear open the Candle Bay Imperial Prison.}}
* {{spoiler|book=ee-sea-01-of-sea-and-shadow|'''Ch 25''' — Elderspawn are monsters that include the Children of Nakothi.}}
* {{spoiler|book=ee-sea-01-of-sea-and-shadow|'''Ch 25''' — The Elderspawn has six black eyes and gills.}}
* {{spoiler|book=ee-sea-01-of-sea-and-shadow|'''Ch 25''' — The island is crawling with an army of Elderspawn.}}
* {{spoiler|book=ee-sea-01-of-sea-and-shadow|'''Ch 26''' — The Emperor is compared to Elderspawn due to his detachment from humanity.}}
* {{spoiler|book=ee-sea-01-of-sea-and-shadow|'''Ch 27''' — Elderspawn are creatures with unfathomable faces.}}

== Of Shadow and Sea ==
* {{spoiler|book=ee-shadow-01-of-shadow-and-sea|'''Ch 4''' — Elderspawn are entities that roving bands of street gangs resemble in the poorer quarters.}}
* {{spoiler|book=ee-shadow-01-of-shadow-and-sea|'''Ch 5''' — The prisoner was once mistaken for Elderspawn.}}
* {{spoiler|book=ee-shadow-01-of-shadow-and-sea|'''Ch 6''' — A giant Elderspawn is tied to the hull of the Navigator's ship.}}
* {{spoiler|book=ee-shadow-01-of-shadow-and-sea|'''Ch 8''' — Elderspawn are chained beneath the ship.}}
* {{spoiler|book=ee-shadow-01-of-shadow-and-sea|'''Ch 10''' — Elderspawn tore apart the man killed by the Emperor.}}
* {{spoiler|book=ee-shadow-01-of-shadow-and-sea|'''Ch 10''' — Using parts of Elderspawn in a ship's construction allows it to guide the captain around hazards of the deep Aion.}}
* {{spoiler|book=ee-shadow-01-of-shadow-and-sea|'''Ch 10''' — Elderspawn were not seen on the way to the island.}}
* {{spoiler|book=ee-shadow-01-of-shadow-and-sea|'''Ch 15''' — An Elderspawn was likely the messenger sent by the Sleepless.}}
* {{spoiler|book=ee-shadow-01-of-shadow-and-sea|'''Ch 17''' — Calder Marten has captive Elderspawn.}}
* {{spoiler|book=ee-shadow-01-of-shadow-and-sea|'''Ch 19''' — Elderspawn are creatures that a second blade might be needed to kill.}}
* {{spoiler|book=ee-shadow-01-of-shadow-and-sea|'''Ch 23''' — An Elderspawn is a creature with dark green skin and small wings.}}
* {{spoiler|book=ee-shadow-01-of-shadow-and-sea|'''Ch 25''' — Calder Marten's orange-and-black sword has an effect on Elderspawn.}}
* {{spoiler|book=ee-shadow-01-of-shadow-and-sea|'''Ch 25''' — Elderspawn are creatures that can flare wings and have tentacles writhing over their mouths.}}
* {{spoiler|book=ee-shadow-01-of-shadow-and-sea|'''Ch 26''' — Lucan fought against the Elderspawn.}}
* {{spoiler|book=ee-shadow-01-of-shadow-and-sea|'''Ch 26''' — Every Elderspawn on the island has vanished.}}
* {{spoiler|book=ee-shadow-01-of-shadow-and-sea|'''Ch 26''' — Elderspawn are creatures that can be swept off the Island by Bastion's Veil.}}
* {{spoiler|book=ee-shadow-01-of-shadow-and-sea|'''Ch 27''' — Meia has killed a giant Elderspawn with her bare hands.}}

== Of Dawn and Darkness ==
* {{spoiler|book=ee-sea-02-of-dawn-and-darkness|'''Ch 3''' — Elderspawn are used as messengers.}}
* {{spoiler|book=ee-sea-02-of-dawn-and-darkness|'''Ch 4''' — Reading an Elderspawn directly can cause madness.}}
* {{spoiler|book=ee-sea-02-of-dawn-and-darkness|'''Ch 6''' — Elderspawn rose all over the world during the Long Mourning.}}
* {{spoiler|book=ee-sea-02-of-dawn-and-darkness|'''Ch 6''' — Elderspawn are in the courtyard outside the Emperor's quarters.}}
* {{spoiler|book=ee-sea-02-of-dawn-and-darkness|'''Ch 9''' — An Elderspawn invasion spread a disease that turned people into monsters.}}
* {{spoiler|book=ee-sea-02-of-dawn-and-darkness|'''Ch 10''' — Elderspawn gore was found in the courtyard.}}
* {{spoiler|book=ee-sea-02-of-dawn-and-darkness|'''Ch 11''' — Elderspawn exist in a perpetual state of malice and burning frustration.}}
* {{spoiler|book=ee-sea-02-of-dawn-and-darkness|'''Ch 11''' — Elderspawn have been sighted in Dylia, Vandenyas, the Nire, and the Capital.}}
* {{spoiler|book=ee-sea-02-of-dawn-and-darkness|'''Ch 11''' — Elderspawn are entities capable of emptying villages.}}
* {{spoiler|book=ee-sea-02-of-dawn-and-darkness|'''Ch 11''' — Elderspawn have chitin that is dark blue or slick purple.}}
* {{spoiler|book=ee-sea-02-of-dawn-and-darkness|'''Ch 12''' — Elderspawn are described similarly to wild lions.}}
* {{spoiler|book=ee-sea-02-of-dawn-and-darkness|'''Ch 13''' — Elderspawn are entities that prisoners are fed to.}}
* {{spoiler|book=ee-sea-02-of-dawn-and-darkness|'''Ch 13''' — Elderspawn have ten spider-legs.}}
* {{spoiler|book=ee-sea-02-of-dawn-and-darkness|'''Ch 13''' — Elderspawn have spear-sharp legs.}}
* {{spoiler|book=ee-sea-02-of-dawn-and-darkness|'''Ch 13''' — Elderspawn are creatures that screech on the shore of Silverreach.}}
* {{spoiler|book=ee-sea-02-of-dawn-and-darkness|'''Ch 14''' — Fleshy Elderspawn fought in the courtyard of the Emperor's chambers.}}
* {{spoiler|book=ee-sea-02-of-dawn-and-darkness|'''Ch 17''' — Elderspawn can imitate human voices.}}
* {{spoiler|book=ee-sea-02-of-dawn-and-darkness|'''Ch 19''' — Calder has a pet Elderspawn.}}
* {{spoiler|book=ee-sea-02-of-dawn-and-darkness|'''Ch 23''' — Elderspawn include Shuffles, who have little intelligence more than animals.}}
* {{spoiler|book=ee-sea-02-of-dawn-and-darkness|'''Ch 23''' — Elderspawn are hordes that can kneel.}}

== Of Darkness and Dawn ==
* {{spoiler|book=ee-shadow-02-of-darkness-and-dawn|'''Ch 2''' — The deaths of Elderspawn produce floating green lights.}}
* {{spoiler|book=ee-shadow-02-of-darkness-and-dawn|'''Ch 2''' — Kerian swept an army of Elderspawn from the shores by collapsing the Veil.}}
* {{spoiler|book=ee-shadow-02-of-darkness-and-dawn|'''Ch 4''' — There was a battle with the Elderspawn.}}
* {{spoiler|book=ee-shadow-02-of-darkness-and-dawn|'''Ch 6''' — The girls survived a meeting with an Elderspawn.}}
* {{spoiler|book=ee-shadow-02-of-darkness-and-dawn|'''Ch 7''' — There is a possibility of a fight with Elderspawn.}}
* {{spoiler|book=ee-shadow-02-of-darkness-and-dawn|'''Ch 8''' — The Guard leader identifies Darius as an Elderspawn.}}
* {{spoiler|book=ee-shadow-02-of-darkness-and-dawn|'''Ch 9''' — Elderspawn created the bronze door in the tunnel.}}
* {{spoiler|book=ee-shadow-02-of-darkness-and-dawn|'''Ch 9''' — An Elderspawn is a creature that moves with a shifting, skittering movement.}}
* {{spoiler|book=ee-shadow-02-of-darkness-and-dawn|'''Ch 9''' — The Elderspawn creature is a ten-legged spider-like entity with a dark purple, almost black, hairy body.}}
* {{spoiler|book=ee-shadow-02-of-darkness-and-dawn|'''Ch 9''' — The Elderspawn creature has eyes on stalks positioned on its back.}}
* {{spoiler|book=ee-shadow-02-of-darkness-and-dawn|'''Ch 9''' — Elderspawn are creatures that do not care about humans.}}
* {{spoiler|book=ee-shadow-02-of-darkness-and-dawn|'''Ch 16''' — Elderspawn have sickening purple blood and death-screams that sound like a blend of a screaming horse and a whistling teakettle.}}
* {{spoiler|book=ee-shadow-02-of-darkness-and-dawn|'''Ch 16''' — Elderspawn are being cleansed from the Capital.}}
* {{spoiler|book=ee-shadow-02-of-darkness-and-dawn|'''Ch 18''' — An Elderspawn is a creature more hideous than any Kameira.}}
* {{spoiler|book=ee-shadow-02-of-darkness-and-dawn|'''Ch 19''' — Elderspawn are beings that can borrow a person's face.}}

== Of Kings and Killers ==
* {{spoiler|book=ee-sea-03-of-kings-and-killers|'''Ch 5''' — Elderspawn messengers translate the voices of the Sleepless cabal.}}
* {{spoiler|book=ee-sea-03-of-kings-and-killers|'''Ch 5''' — Elderspawn report back to the Sleepless leaders.}}
* {{spoiler|book=ee-sea-03-of-kings-and-killers|'''Ch 6''' — Estyr identified something or someone as an Elderspawn.}}
* {{spoiler|book=ee-sea-03-of-kings-and-killers|'''Ch 9''' — Silver rings are used to ward off Elderspawn.}}
* {{spoiler|book=ee-sea-03-of-kings-and-killers|'''Ch 10''' — Elderspawn may be encircling the tower.}}
* {{spoiler|book=ee-sea-03-of-kings-and-killers|'''Ch 12''' — Giant fanged Elderspawn are in the surf.}}
* {{spoiler|book=ee-sea-03-of-kings-and-killers|'''Ch 12''' — Elderspawn are lime-green creatures with six yellow eyes and a dozen webbed amphibian feet.}}
* {{spoiler|book=ee-sea-03-of-kings-and-killers|'''Ch 12''' — Elderspawn have stingers.}}
* {{spoiler|book=ee-sea-03-of-kings-and-killers|'''Ch 14''' — Elderspawn are giant flatworms with extendible jaws and crocodile teeth.}}
* {{spoiler|book=ee-sea-03-of-kings-and-killers|'''Ch 14''' — The Elderspawn have affected Mister Goss's mind and body.}}
* {{spoiler|book=ee-sea-03-of-kings-and-killers|'''Ch 15''' — Calder mentions having seen Elderspawn in his speech.}}
* {{spoiler|book=ee-sea-03-of-kings-and-killers|'''Ch 16''' — Elderspawn are creations of Othaghor.}}
* {{spoiler|book=ee-sea-03-of-kings-and-killers|'''Ch 17''' — Some Pilgrims gain a holy zeal toward the eradication of Elderspawn due to the Awakening process.}}
* {{spoiler|book=ee-sea-03-of-kings-and-killers|'''Ch 19''' — Elderspawn served Jerri by dragging food and disposing of waste during her imprisonment.}}
* {{spoiler|book=ee-sea-03-of-kings-and-killers|'''Ch 20''' — Elderspawn are servants of Elders.}}
* {{spoiler|book=ee-sea-03-of-kings-and-killers|'''Ch 20''' — Elderspawn are creatures that exist around the Capital.}}
* {{spoiler|book=ee-sea-03-of-kings-and-killers|'''Ch 21''' — Ordinary people in the Capital invested objects to protect them from Elderspawn.}}
* {{spoiler|book=ee-sea-03-of-kings-and-killers|'''Ch 24''' — A mass of aquatic Elderspawn seethed beneath the surface.}}
* {{spoiler|book=ee-sea-03-of-kings-and-killers|'''Ch 24''' — Elderspawn are fanged fish with clawed, stubby legs and arms.}}
* {{spoiler|book=ee-sea-03-of-kings-and-killers|'''Ch 24''' — The water was dark with Elderspawn corpses.}}
* {{spoiler|book=ee-sea-03-of-kings-and-killers|'''Ch 24''' — Calder snaps Elderspawn as he marches forward.}}
* {{spoiler|book=ee-sea-03-of-kings-and-killers|'''Ch 25''' — Elderspawn are entities that the Awakened sword was designed to be used against.}}
* {{spoiler|book=ee-sea-03-of-kings-and-killers|'''Ch 25''' — Pieces of Elderspawn and human corpses were stitching themselves together.}}

== Of Killers and Kings ==
* {{spoiler|book=ee-shadow-03-of-killers-and-kings|'''Ch 5''' — Some Elderspawn share a mind like a hive.}}
* {{spoiler|book=ee-shadow-03-of-killers-and-kings|'''Ch 6''' — Estyr uses the term 'Elderspawn' as a declaration of hatred and fury.}}
* {{spoiler|book=ee-shadow-03-of-killers-and-kings|'''Ch 10''' — Allbright suggests flushing out the Elderspawn from the Imperial Palace.}}
* {{spoiler|book=ee-shadow-03-of-killers-and-kings|'''Ch 13''' — Elderspawn cannot pass through the Bastion’s Veil mist.}}
* {{spoiler|book=ee-shadow-03-of-killers-and-kings|'''Ch 14''' — There was an uprising of Elderspawn around the world on the night of the Emperor’s death.}}
* {{spoiler|book=ee-shadow-03-of-killers-and-kings|'''Ch 18''' — Loreli's Beacon light torches Elderspawn.}}
* {{spoiler|book=ee-shadow-03-of-killers-and-kings|'''Ch 20''' — Crafting Elderspawn is a way the Creeping Shadow can influence the world.}}
* {{spoiler|book=ee-shadow-03-of-killers-and-kings|'''Ch 22''' — Some Elder tombs were driven out by Elderspawn.}}
* {{spoiler|book=ee-shadow-03-of-killers-and-kings|'''Ch 23''' — Elderspawn pooled around Darius Allbright's feet and clung to walls.}}
* {{spoiler|book=ee-shadow-03-of-killers-and-kings|'''Ch 25''' — An army of Elderspawn was seen in the distance.}}
* {{spoiler|book=ee-shadow-03-of-killers-and-kings|'''Ch 25''' — Elderspawn are in the water around the Navigators.}}
* {{spoiler|book=ee-shadow-03-of-killers-and-kings|'''Ch 25''' — Elderspawn were on the deck.}}
* {{spoiler|book=ee-shadow-03-of-killers-and-kings|'''Ch 26''' — Elderspawn of flesh and bone can crawl up railings.}}

[[Category:Timelines]]
